role_title: Product Manager, Claude Code Model Performance

seniority.level: Senior IC (likely Senior PM / Staff PM)
level_signals: Comp range $305K-$460K base — top of range consistent with Staff-level at Anthropic
Expected to own model launch end-to-end and drive research team direction
Role requires influencing frontier research team and defining eval roadmap

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have personally built agentic evals (e.g. SWE-bench-style task suites)
Are a daily Claude Code user and can articulate what behaviors you'd want to change or add to the model
Have an engineering background and 2+ years in product management, or equivalent experience driving product direction as an engineer
Have a deep grasp of AI concepts and are comfortable going deep on model behavior, prompt engineering, and evaluation methodology
Are a systems thinker: when you find a problem, you build the infrastructure that prevents its whole class
Have launched products or capabilities in ambiguous, research-adjacent environments
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have a creative, hacker spirit and love solving puzzles

scope.surface: Claude Code model performance — model launches, agentic evals, coding capability improvements
scope.business_model: developer-facing AI coding agent, mixed B2C and B2B
core_responsibilities: Own model launch planning and execution for Claude Code: define readiness criteria, coordinate across research and product engineering, and ensure launches land cleanly with developers
Design and implement agentic evals that measure real-world coding performance
Drive the engineering team's eval roadmap
Partner with researchers working on coding capabilities to define target behaviors and influence model development with evidence from real usage
Talk with users and analyze transcripts to understand capability gaps and turn research progress into shipped improvements
Synthesize signal from internal users, external developers, and competitive benchmarks into clear priorities

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($305K-$460K base)
Clear ownership: model launch end-to-end and eval roadmap
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Strong product surface clarity — Claude Code model performance is a well-defined scope
Direct partnership with frontier researchers signals high-impact role
red_flags: Hard requirement to have 'personally built agentic evals' is a narrow bar that eliminates most PMs
San Francisco and Seattle only — no remote option, eliminates distributed candidates
Wide comp range ($305K-$460K) makes it difficult to assess target level without additional context
Years of experience minimum is stated as 2+ years PM but comp ceiling suggests far more senior candidate is expected — ambiguity in seniority targeting

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly in JD)
ambiguities: The JD lists 'You might be a good fit if you' framing for all requirements, making it formally ambiguous whether they are hard or soft. Given the specificity and conditional phrasing, items have been classified as hard must-haves except for 'hacker spirit' which is clearly cultural. This follows rule 3 guidance.

Minimum years of experience is stated as '2+ years in product management' but the compensation ceiling of $460K is consistent with a Staff-level or principal candidate — the role may target a wide band. Flagged as ambiguity.
The education field of study requirement ('A field relevant to the role as demonstrated through coursework, training, or professional experience') is vague and not mapped to a specific discipline.
